name: Grounding reads exclude draft / superseded / archived
query: What does the manual say about the deployment pipeline? (grounding read for planning)
context: The wiki has a canonical 'Deployment' page, plus a competing draft revision and an older superseded page on the same topic.
expected_behavior: Resolves the living manual node (root + IA path) for the concept.
For a GROUNDING read, status-filters to canonical — EXCLUDES draft / superseded / archived so a quarantined body never poisons reasoning.
If a candidate is superseded, follows superseded_by and reads the live successor instead.
Uses docmost-cli reads only (page get / search --cached); never reads the SQLite cache files directly.
In spec mode (wiki-first), instead resolves the INTENT node for the story being built.
success_criteria: The draft and superseded pages did not contribute to the grounding answer.
Only currently-true canonical content was used (P6).
A superseded hit was redirected to its live successor.
